items.q: Which colours typically advance in a composition?
items.why: Students conflate darkness with advancement. In atmospheric perspective, COOLER colours recede (think distant blue mountains) and warmer colours advance, regardless of darkness.

items.q: What is the significance of Duchamp's 'Fountain'?
items.correct: It challenged the definition of art - the artist's choice, not their skill, becomes the artwork.
items.random_wrong: It is a study of plumbing.
items.why: Students miss the conceptual point. Duchamp didn't make the urinal - he selected and presented it. The radical claim is that ART IS DESIGNATION, opening the door to all conceptual art.

items.correct: The small projecting line at the end of letter strokes.
items.misconception: A whole typeface that looks formal.
items.why: Students conflate the FEATURE (the small foot/stroke) with the CLASSIFICATION (serif typefaces, which have them). Sans serif means 'without serifs' - those little projections at letter endings.

items.q: How many resolved artworks does an IBDP HL exhibition require minimum?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 8. “10” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Whose 1976 essay critiques the white cube gallery?
items.why: Correct answer: O'Doherty. “Krauss” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Documenta first open in Kassel?
items.why: The real figure is 1955 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
